reason: Both answers hit all the key points equally well: Goodhart's law framing, flat/roughly-constant activated users despite 2x signups, pay-on-activation as the primary fix, segmenting referred vs organic to rule out confounds, fraud/cap controls, and reframing success metric as cost-per-activated-user rather than cost-per-signup. Neither contains factual or reasoning errors. They are structurally nearly identical with only superficial wording differences (A frames the final check as CAC comparison, B as channel comparison — equivalent). No meaningful quality gap.
